evals.prompt: Generate a complete production-ready EKS Terraform project for a customer in us-west-2. Requirements: EKS 1.30, Karpenter for autoscaling, AWS Load Balancer Controller, CoreDNS, VPC-CNI with prefix delegation enabled, and cert-manager. The cluster should use private endpoint access only with VPC endpoints for ECR, S3, STS, and SSM. Use IRSA for all addon service accounts. Output the full project structure.
evals.expected_output: A complete Terraform project with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf, and addon configurations. Includes EKS module invocation with private endpoint, VPC endpoint resources, IRSA roles for each addon, Karpenter NodePool and EC2NodeClass, and proper dependency ordering with two-phase webhook handling for cert-manager.
expectations: Generates a versions.tf with required_providers block pinning aws, kubernetes, and helm provider versions TODO: human review
Creates IRSA IAM roles with proper trust policies for at least aws-load-balancer-controller and Karpenter TODO: human review
Configures EKS cluster with endpoint_private_access = true and endpoint_public_access = false TODO: human review
Includes VPC endpoint resources for at minimum ecr.api, ecr.dkr, s3, sts TODO: human review
Karpenter configuration includes an EC2NodeClass and at least one NodePool resource TODO: human review
Uses two-phase module architecture or separate webhook addon module to handle dependency ordering between cert-manager and LBC TODO: human review
Sets before_compute = true for vpc-cni or eks-pod-identity-agent to prevent NodeCreationFailure TODO: human review
Uses terraform-aws-modules/eks community module as the cluster foundation (not raw aws_eks_cluster resource) TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Must implement two-phase module architecture splitting webhook addons (LBC) from remaining addons
vpc-cni and eks-pod-identity-agent must set before_compute: true to prevent NodeCreationFailure
Must use the terraform-aws-modules/eks community module as the EKS cluster foundation
VPC-CNI prefix delegation must be explicitly enabled for increased pod density
Karpenter must use the dedicated submodule from terraform-aws-modules/eks, not eks-blueprints-addons
Karpenter requires discovery tags on subnets and security groups
Karpenter configuration requires an EC2NodeClass CRD for node provisioning settings
Karpenter configuration requires at least one NodePool CRD defining instance requirements
Production cluster must use private endpoint access
Production cluster must disable public endpoint
Private clusters require VPC endpoints for ECR API and ECR DKR
LBC requires explicit vpcId in Helm values because IMDS fallback fails with hop-limit restrictions
Terraform projects must declare required_providers with version constraints
Node launch templates must enforce IMDSv2 (http_tokens = required) for security
EBS volumes must use gp3 (never gp2) for cost and performance
Multus CNI must never be enabled due to thick-plugin pod-lookup race condition
kube2iam is deprecated; IRSA or Pod Identity must be used instead
OpenTofu must not be recommended due to severe depends_on performance regression

evals.prompt: Add Kyverno and external-secrets-operator as custom addons to my existing EKS Terraform project. Kyverno needs two-phase webhook ordering (install CRDs first, then policies). External-secrets needs IRSA with access to AWS Secrets Manager. The project uses Pattern 2a (ArgoCD managing addons). Generate the ArgoCD Application manifests and any supporting Terraform for IAM.
evals.expected_output: ArgoCD Application YAML manifests for both Kyverno and external-secrets-operator, plus Terraform code for the external-secrets IRSA role. Kyverno uses two-phase ordering with a separate CRD Application that has a lower sync-wave. External-secrets Application references the IRSA role ARN via values override.
expectations: Generates separate ArgoCD Application manifests for Kyverno CRDs and Kyverno policies with sync-wave annotations ensuring CRDs deploy first TODO: human review
Creates an IAM role with trust policy for the external-secrets-operator service account with secretsmanager:GetSecretValue permission TODO: human review
ArgoCD Application for external-secrets passes the IRSA role ARN as a Helm values override TODO: human review
Both addons specify a target namespace and create-namespace sync option TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Kyverno CRDs must deploy before policies using ArgoCD sync-wave annotations
Kyverno ArgoCD syncOptions must use Replace=true, NOT ServerSideApply=true which conflicts with selfHeal
ServerSideApply=true must not be used with Kyverno because it conflicts with ArgoCD selfHeal + --force
External-secrets IRSA policy must grant access to AWS Secrets Manager
IRSA requires the service account annotation eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn or passing role ARN via Helm values
ArgoCD Applications should use CreateNamespace sync option for addon namespaces
ArgoCD Application manifests must use the argoproj.io API group
IRSA trust policies require the OIDC provider and sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity
ArgoCD syncPolicy should enable selfHeal for drift detection and remediation
kube2iam is deprecated; IRSA must be used for pod-level IAM
Kyverno requires separate CRD installation before policy deployment

evals.prompt: I need to choose between Pattern 1 (full Terraform), Pattern 2a (ArgoCD+Terraform), and Pattern 2b (ArgoCD+ACK/KRO) for my customer. They have: 5 EKS clusters across 3 environments, a platform team of 2 people, no existing GitOps tooling, and a requirement for SOC2 compliance with drift detection. Generate the project scaffold for your recommended pattern.
evals.expected_output: Recommendation of Pattern 2a with justification based on team size, multi-cluster needs, and compliance requirements. Generates a project scaffold with Terraform for base infrastructure and ArgoCD ApplicationSets for addon management across environments, including drift detection via ArgoCD sync status.
expectations: Recommends Pattern 2a (or provides a clear rationale if choosing differently) based on the stated constraints of small team and drift detection needs TODO: human review

If Pattern 2a chosen, generates a directory structure separating base infrastructure Terraform from ArgoCD addon manifests. If Pattern 1 chosen, generates multi-environment Terraform structure with separate tfvars per environment TODO: human review

If Pattern 2a chosen, includes an ArgoCD ApplicationSet with a git or list generator to target multiple clusters/environments. If Pattern 1 chosen, uses Terraform workspaces or per-environment directories to manage multiple clusters TODO: human review
Addresses SOC2 compliance by incorporating drift detection mechanism such as ArgoCD sync status, terraform plan -detailed-exitcode in CI, or OPA/Kyverno policies TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Must explicitly recommend a pattern (1, 2a, or 2b) with justification based on team size, GitOps maturity, and compliance needs
If Pattern 2a: uses ArgoCD ApplicationSets for multi-cluster management. If Pattern 1: uses Terraform for_each, workspaces, or per-environment directories
If Pattern 2a: drift detection via ArgoCD selfHeal/auto-sync. If Pattern 1: drift detection via scheduled terraform plan with -detailed-exitcode
If Pattern 2a: uses GitOps Bridge to pass Terraform metadata to ArgoCD. If Pattern 1: uses remote state or tfvars for environment separation
vpc-cni and eks-pod-identity-agent must set before_compute: true regardless of pattern
All patterns use terraform-aws-modules/eks for the EKS cluster infrastructure
Must use eks-blueprints-addons module (Pattern 2a for IRSA roles, Pattern 1 for full addon management) or native EKS cluster_addons
SOC2 compliance requires drift detection — ArgoCD sync status (Pattern 2a) or scheduled terraform plan with -detailed-exitcode (Pattern 1)
Multi-environment projects should use per-environment tfvars files for configuration separation
Must reference the EKS cluster identity (ARN, endpoint, or name) for multi-cluster targeting
OpenTofu must not be recommended due to severe depends_on performance regression (30+ min plan)
kube2iam is deprecated; IRSA or Pod Identity must be used
Terraform projects must declare required_providers with version constraints in versions.tf

turns.answer: Mixed x86 instances, m5.xlarge and m5.2xlarge
